--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

The attached description is only a description of
the scope of services to
be provided; it is not the full agreement. Please
review the scope and let me
know if it is consistent with your understanding of
what EDSA is supposed to
do for you. I will work with John to make sure that
the terms of the
agreement under which the scope of services is to be
performed is acceptable.

Darren K. Indyke
Attorney-At-Law
457 Madison Avenue
4th Floor
New York, New York 10022

Fax: 212-750-0381

law.

From: "John Amerling" jamerling@rrcaribbean.com
To: dkiesq@aol.com, "Darren Indyke"
dkiesq@aol.com
Subject: EDSA
Date: Wed, 6 Sep 2006 15:36:50 -0400

I have revised the scope of work for EDSA. I had
difficulty figuring out
what they were going to do for the lump sum amount.
What we need them to do
is in the enclosed scope. I want to get Mr. Behling
and EDSA on the same
page with us using this document, but I do not want
to do so without having
your review since you did the initial work on the
contract.
